key_themes: Q1 earnings beat with raised 2026 guidance
Strong oncology/innovative medicine momentum and pipeline progress
Widespread analyst upgrades and higher price targets
Stelara patent cliff offset by dividend reliability
summary: Johnson & Johnson delivered a solid Q1 2026 beat (revenue $24.1B, adj. EPS $2.70) and raised full-year guidance to ~$100.8B sales and $11.55 EPS midpoint, driven by oncology strength (Darzalex, Erleada, Carvykti) and new approvals, prompting numerous analyst PT hikes to $250–$283 with Buy ratings. Persistent talc lawsuits (60k+ cases, ongoing mediation and rejected bankruptcy attempts) and a ~59–60% Stelara sales drop from biosimilars created a muted stock reaction and remain key risks, yet JNJ is widely viewed as a defensive dividend aristocrat (64th increase announced) with a filling pipeline. Overall investor and analyst sentiment is positive for long-term holding despite near-term legal and product-specific pressures.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/JNJ/)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/JNJ)[[3]](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JNJ)
sources: 15+ recent news articles (Yahoo Finance, CNBC, TipRanks, Seeking Alpha, MarketWatch, Motley Fool), 20+ relevant X posts (earnings reaction, analyst notes, investor commentary from April 2026)
